name: Torre de Belém from Rua Augusta — route in Lisboa
name_original: Torre de Belém a partir da Rua Augusta — percurso em Lisboa
region_intro: Jogging around Santa Justa offers diverse terrain, from river valleys to more challenging mountain ascents. The region is characterized by ancient geological formations and varied woodlands, providing a rich natural backdrop for outdoor pursuits. Many running routes follow the Ferreira and Sousa rivers, leading through picturesque valleys and serene oak groves. The Serra de Santa Justa is part of the Park Serras do Porto, classified as a Protected Landscape. Best jogging routes around Santa Justa The most popular jogging route is Marquis of Pombal Square – Aqueduto das Águas Livres loop from Rossio, a 12.1 miles (19.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Aqueduto das Águas Livres – Marquis of Pombal Square loop from Rossio, a difficult 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path. This route offers varied terrain with notable climbs. Local runners also love the Torre de Belém from Rua Augusta — route in Lisboa, a 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail leading through urban and riverside areas, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Jogging around Santa Justa is defined by river valleys, oak groves, and diverse woodlands, with options for both casual joggers and those seeking more strenuous runs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to difficult ascents. The routes in Santa Justa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 45 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Santa Justa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Santa Justa?
A: Santa Justa offers a diverse network of over 20 dedicated running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, including 2 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 3 more challenging options for experienced runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Santa Justa?
A: The Serra de Santa Justa boasts a highly varied terrain, perfect for jogging. You'll find everything from tranquil riverbank paths along the Ferreira and Sousa rivers, leading through picturesque oak groves, to more challenging mountain ascents offering panoramic views. The area is also characterized by ancient geological formations and diverse woodlands.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running paths in Santa Justa?
A: Yes, Santa Justa has options suitable for beginners. There are 2 easy running routes available, perfect for those looking for a gentle jog or a warm-up. These paths typically feature less elevation gain and smoother surfaces, allowing for a comfortable experience.

Q: What are some challenging running routes in Santa Justa for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Santa Justa offers 3 difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32280445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marquis of Pombal Square – Aqueduto das Águas Livres loop from Rossio&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.1-mile (19.4 km) trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding path is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5301537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduto das Águas Livres – Marquis of Pombal Square loop from Rossio&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.0-mile (17.7 km) route with varied terrain and notable climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the running routes in Santa Justa?
A: Jogging in Santa Justa allows you to explore a rich natural and historical landscape. You can enjoy picturesque river valleys, serene oak groves, and ancient geological formations. Some routes offer panoramic views from higher elevations. Additionally, you might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8190638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from the Rua Augusta Arch&lt;/a&gt;, the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/215641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Justa Lift&lt;/a&gt;, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/444223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Commerce Square&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Santa Justa?
A: Many of the running routes in Santa Justa are designed as loops, offering convenient circular paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23678747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rossio Railway Station – View from the Rua Augusta Arch loop from Glória - Restauradores&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.5 km) circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32359146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rua Augusta Arch – Lisbon Cruise Port loop from Rossio&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.9-mile (7.8 km) trail.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Santa Justa?
A: The running routes in Santa Justa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 45 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from peaceful river valleys to challenging mountain ascents, and the rich natural backdrop provided by the ancient geological formations and varied woodlands. The extensive network of trails offers options for all abilities, making it a popular choice for many.

Q: Is Santa Justa a good place for family-friendly running or walking?
A: While the guide focuses on jogging, the diverse terrain of Santa Justa, including river valleys and oak groves, offers paths that can be enjoyed by families. The 2 easy routes are particularly suitable for a more relaxed pace, which could be ideal for families looking to combine running with a leisurely walk in nature. The region's protected landscape also provides a safe and scenic environment.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Santa Justa?
A: The natural environment of the Serra de Santa Justa, with its extensive trail network and diverse woodlands, is generally welcoming for dogs. While specific dog-friendly designations for each route are not provided, many outdoor trails in Portugal allow well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always advisable to check local regulations or signage at trailheads regarding pets.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Santa Justa?
A: Santa Justa offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures, making them ideal for outdoor activities. Even in summer, the oak groves and river valleys can offer some shade. Winter jogging is also possible, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler. The diverse terrain ensures there's always a suitable route regardless of the season.

Q: Are there any urban running routes in Santa Justa?
A: Yes, some routes in the Santa Justa area combine natural beauty with urban exploration. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381183015/torre-de-belem-from-rua-augusta-route-in-lisboa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Belém from Rua Augusta — route in Lisboa&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.5-mile (18.5 km) trail that leads through both urban and riverside areas, offering a different kind of jogging experience.
tips: The Arch of Rua Augusta is the unmistakable entrance to the Plaza del Comercio from the street that bears the same name - the main artery of the Baixa - and had been closed for some time due to remodeling works in which the Lisbon Tourism Association was he has spent almost a million Euros.
The bridge with a view of Christ the Redeemer is just one highlight of many that can be discovered on the riverbank between Belém and Lisbon city center. The best way is by bike.

The Monument to the Discoveries, popularly known as Padrão dos Descobrimentos, is a monument built in 1960, on the banks of the Tagus River, in Belém, Lisbon, to commemorate the 500th anniversary of the death of Henry the Navigator.

The Torre de Belem is the most characteristic building in Lisbon, being the monument in Portugal that comes closest to instant recognition. This delightfully tiny fortress has guarded the Tagus Estuary since the 16th century and was built in the heyday of the Manueline style of architecture.

waypoints.name: View from the Rua Augusta Arch
